symbols.name: \(\epsilon\)
symbols.def: Uniform lower bound on each treatment propensity.

symbols.name: \(c\)
symbols.def: Fixed constant in the regime \(d\le c n\log n\).

symbols.name: \(r=(r_{ay})_{a,y\in\{0,1\}}\)
symbols.def: Exponent vector for a four-cell polynomial monomial, with \( |r|=\sum_{a,y}r_{ay}\).

symbols.name: \(\widehat T_{\mathcal L}\)
symbols.def: Sum of the factorial-moment polynomial contributions over \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n\) in \(def:hybrid-estimator-handle\).

definitions.name: unrestricted discrete-confounder experiment class
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}:=\{P^{\otimes n}: P\in\mathsf{Prob}(\{1,\ldots,d\}\times\{0,1\}^2),\ n,d\in\mathbb N,\ 0<\epsilon\le1/2,\text{ and }P\text{ satisfies }\mathrm{ass:overlap}\}\).

definitions.name: universally tuned balanced two-split ratio-polynomial hybrid estimator
definitions.construction:
Set \(I_0=\{1,\ldots,\lfloor n/2\rfloor\}\), \(I_1=\{\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1,\ldots,n\}\), \(m_j=|I_j|\), and \(L=\log(en)\). Set \(A=6\), and put \(\lambda_0=256\), \(b_0=4096\), \(D_A=8\log(9A/8)=8\log(27/4)\), and
\[
\alpha_0=\min\left\{1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}\right\}.
\]
For \(n\ge2\), put \(M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\}\) and \(B(n)=b_0L/m_1\). Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\), \(\alpha_0L\ge2\), \(4M(n)^2\le m_1\), and \(4M(n)/m_1\le3B(n)/4\). For \(n\ge N_0\), abbreviate \(M=M(n)\), \(B=B(n)\), and set
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n=\left\{k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L\right\},\qquad \widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
Put \(N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky}\), \(N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky}\), and
\[
\widehat h_k=\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}\left\{\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}-\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}\right\}.
\]
Define \(H_M(x)=\{1-T_M(1-2x)\}/(2M^2)\), \(E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x\), and \(G_M(x)=\{1-E_M(x)\}/x\), using polynomial continuation at zero, and
\[
P_{M,B}(u)=\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)-\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right).
\]
Here \(s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1}\), \(s(u)=s_0(u)+s_1(u)\), \(t_1(u)=u_{11}\), and \(t_0(u)=u_{01}\). Expand \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), set
\[
\widehat P_k=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_r\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal H}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal L}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k,
\]
and define \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}=\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}\). For \(n<N_0\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The estimator uses no overlap constant and is computable in \(O(dM^4)\) arithmetic operations.

definitions.name: minimax ATE risk
definitions.construction: \(\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}:=\inf_{\widehat\tau}\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau-\tau(P))^2]\), where the infimum is over measurable estimators based on \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\).

definitions.name: two-category confounding witness
definitions.construction: For \(d=2\), set \(p_1=p_2=1/2\), \(\pi_1=\epsilon\), \(\pi_2=1-\epsilon\), \(\mu_{01}=\mu_{02}=0\), \(\mu_{11}=1\), and \(\mu_{12}=0\). Define a full-data extension by drawing \((Y(0),Y(1))=(0,\mathbf 1\{X=1\})\) and then drawing \(A\mid X=k\sim\mathsf{Bernoulli}(\pi_k)\).

statements.statement: The full-data law in \(def:two-category-witness\) satisfies \(ass:consistency\), \(ass:conditional-exchangeability\), and \(ass:overlap\), has \(\tau(P)=1/2\), and has naive observed contrast \(E_P[Y\mid A=1]-E_P[Y\mid A=0]=\epsilon\).
statements.justification: The full-data coupling verifies consistency, conditional exchangeability, overlap, the ATE, and the observed contrast exactly.
statements.gap: Strict confounding occurs for \(0<\epsilon<1/2\); at \(\epsilon=1/2\) the witness becomes randomized.

statements.statement: There are constants \(C_\epsilon,c_\epsilon>0\) and a computable calibration of \(def:hybrid-estimator-handle\) such that, for every \(d\le c_\epsilon n\log n\) and every \(P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}\), its light-cell contribution \(\widehat T_{\mathcal L}\) obeys \[ E_P\!\left[\left\{\widehat T_{\mathcal L}-\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\phi(q_k)\right\}^2\right]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2 n}\right\}. \] The calibration uses a polynomial approximation of \(\phi\) on the overlap-restricted four-cell cone and factorial-moment monomials from \(I_1\).
statements.justification: Equations \(1\)--\(7\) derive the exact \(A=6\) Chebyshev certificate, and the remaining argument proves factorial-moment normalization, fixed-multinomial covariance control, approximation bias, and pilot false-light control.

statements.statement: For every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), can the explicitly calibrated ratio-polynomial hybrid in \(def:hybrid-estimator-handle\) satisfy, uniformly over \(d\le c_\epsilon n\log n\), \[c_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}\le\mathsf R_{n,d,\epsilon}\le\sup_{P^{\otimes n}\in\mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}}E_P[(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}-\tau(P))^2]\le C_\epsilon\left\{\frac1n+\frac{d^2}{n^2\log^2n}\right\}?\]
statements.justification: The valid matched question is strict-interior and has complete upper and lower helper nodes.
statements.gap: Formal resolution awaits application of the ratio-polynomial definition and endpoint domain correction.
statements.consumer: The sample-complexity, consistency, and parametric-elbow conclusions.

statements.statement: Building on \(thm:sharp-minimax-fixed-interior\), can an estimator in \(def:adaptive-handle\), without taking \(\epsilon\) as input, attain \(C_\epsilon\{n^{-1}+d^2/(n^2\log^2n)\}\) simultaneously for every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), with universal tuning constants?
statements.justification: For each fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), the candidate grid contains a hybrid attaining the fixed-interior rate.
statements.gap: The frozen risk proxies do not yield an explicit data-only selector or an oracle inequality controlling the candidates' potentially nonmonotone approximation bias.
statements.consumer: A fully implementable estimator that does not take the overlap constant as input.

sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The estimator uses only split-sample joint cell counts and factorial moments; it does not posit a smooth or sparse nuisance model.

bibliography.citation: Zeng, Zhenghao, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Yanjun Han, and Edward H. Kennedy (2026). Causal inference with high-dimensional discrete covariates. arXiv:2405.00118v3.
Jiao, Jiantao, Kartik Venkat, Yanjun Han, and Tsachy Weissman (2015). "Minimax estimation of functionals of discrete distributions." IEEE Transactions on Information Theory 61(5): 2835--2885.

Han, Yanjun, Jiantao Jiao, and Tsachy Weissman (2020). "Minimax rate-optimal estimation of divergences between discrete distributions." IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Information Theory 1(3): 814--832.
Wu, Yihong, and Pengkun Yang (2016). "Minimax rates of entropy estimation on large alphabets via best polynomial approximation." IEEE Transactions on Information Theory 62(6): 3702--3720.
Liu, Lin, Rajarshi Mukherjee, Whitney K. Newey, and James M. Robins (2017). "Semiparametric efficient empirical higher order influence function estimators." arXiv:1705.07577.

tldr: One computable balanced ratio-polynomial hybrid, with universal numerical tuning \(A=6\), \(\lambda_0=256\), and \(b_0=4096\), attains the fixed-interior minimax MSE \(n^{-1}+d^2/(n^2\log^2 n)\) for every fixed \(0<\epsilon<1/2\) without taking \(\epsilon\) as input. Parametric risk holds through \(d=O(\sqrt n\log n)\), and consistency holds exactly for \(d=o(n\log n)\) in the proved range. For every \(n,d\), the centered estimator has MSE at most \(n^{-1}+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2\); at \(\epsilon=1/2\), minimax risk lies between \(1/(100n)\) and \(1/n\), uniformly in \(d\).

project_justification.gap: Zeng, Balakrishnan, Han, and Kennedy prove the strict-interior moment-matching lower scale, while the standard estimators they analyze leave a log-squared upper-bound gap.
project_justification.niche: The missing upper kernel is a coupled four-cell Chebyshev reciprocal polynomial with unbiased fixed-multinomial factorial lifts on pilot-certified light cells, combined with an aggregate ratio estimator on heavy cells. The light-cell proof itself derives the exact \(A=6\) coefficient certificate and normalizes the within- and across-category covariances.
project_justification.fill: The universal balanced two-split hybrid closes the fixed-interior gap. The converse is supplied by the source-backed fixed-sample control-zero lower-bound leaf and transfers because \(\tau=\psi_1\) on that subclass. Separate proved nodes give the all-\(n,d\) centered near-randomization bound \(n^{-1}+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2\), the randomized endpoint bracket \(1/(100n)\le\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le1/n\), and, for \(n\ge N_\epsilon\), the combined upper envelope.

related_work: Zeng, Balakrishnan, Han, and Kennedy, \cite{ZengBalakrishnanHanKennedy2024}, Theorem 2, give the lower scale \(n^{-1}+d^2/(n^2\log^2 n)\) for fixed strict-interior overlap. Their Appendix C.5 gives the control-zero moment-matched construction and the \(d\lesssim n\log n\) range, while Lemma 4 and Appendix D.2 transfer the relaxed Poisson experiment to fixed sample size; on this subclass the treated mean equals the ATE. Jiao, Venkat, Han, and Weissman, \cite{JiaoVenkatHanWeissman2015}, Han, Jiao, and Weissman, \cite{HanJiaoWeissman2020}, and Wu and Yang, \cite{WuYang2016}, develop the large-alphabet architecture of best-polynomial approximation with unbiased factorial-moment lifting for nonsmooth discrete functionals. Higher-order influence-function estimators, such as \cite{LiuMukherjeeNeweyRobins2017}, address a different semiparametric architecture. Those precedents do not supply the coupled four-cell overlap-cone approximation needed here. The contribution proved here is the causal ATE construction: the exact \(A=6\) cone certificate, fixed-multinomial within- and cross-category covariance normalization, pilot false-light control, heavy-cell aggregation, and a single universal numerical calibration that matches the published converse.
interpretation: \section{Results at a glance}\begin{enumerate}\item Theorem~\ref{thm:sharp-minimax-fixed-interior} gives the matched fixed-interior rate, parametric elbow, and consistency frontier.\item Lemma~\ref{lem:light-cell-polynomial} derives the Chebyshev certificate and proves the rare-cell kernel.\item Lemma~\ref{lem:universal-heavy-cell-rate} proves the complementary heavy-cell aggregation bound, and clause \(\mathrm{(i)}\) of Theorem~\ref{thm:overlap-adaptive-universal-hybrid} gives universal fixed-class tuning.\item Lemma~\ref{lem:zeng-one-arm-minimax-lower} records the source-backed fixed-sample control-zero converse, and Lemma~\ref{lem:ate-lower-bound-transfer} transfers it to ATE risk.\item Lemma~\ref{lem:near-randomization-linear-upper} gives the centered all-\(n,d\) bound \(n^{-1}+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2\), and Lemma~\ref{lem:randomized-endpoint-minimax} gives \(1/(100n)\le\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le1/n\) for every \(n,d\).\item Lemma~\ref{lem:combined-upper-envelope} gives the sharpened combined envelope with \(K_\epsilon=\max\{C_\epsilon,4\}\) for \(n\ge N_\epsilon\).\item Proposition~\ref{prop:two-category-confounding} gives the verified confounding witness.\item Clause \(\mathrm{(v)}\) records that the matching triangular-array lower phase remains open.\end{enumerate}
technical_internal_limitation: The fixed-interior constants are not controlled uniformly along triangular arrays \(\epsilon=\epsilon_n\). The combined envelope uses a pointwise hybrid bound valid from \(N_\epsilon\) onward and an all-\(n,d\) centered bound, but no matching lower bound determines the transition as \(\epsilon_n\) approaches \(1/2\) or zero.
honest_scope: Proved are the causal witness, the in-light-proof \(A=6\) coefficient certificate, light and heavy kernels, the single universally tuned hybrid, the matched fixed-interior minimax rate through \(d\lesssim_\epsilon n\log n\), the parametric elbow, the consistency frontier, the centered all-\(n,d\) near-randomization bound \(n^{-1}+4(1/2-\epsilon)^2\), the endpoint bracket \(1/(100n)\le\mathsf R_{n,d,1/2}\le1/n\), and the combined envelope on \(n\ge N_\epsilon\). The lower transfer uses the source-backed control-zero construction, moment-matching range, and fixed-sample transfer in \cite{ZengBalakrishnanHanKennedy2024}. No uniform triangular-array lower phase or sharp overlap-varying constants are claimed.
